source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Modeling Implications in LeanIX
CONTENT:
• Interfaces are central integration points that connect strategic initiatives and operational systems.
• Initiatives may depend on the availability or design of specific interfaces to achieve business goals.
• IT Products consume or provide interfaces, which must be modeled to reflect technical dependencies.
• The diagram supports cross-layer traceability from strategy to execution in LeanIX.

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Definition: Tech Capability
CONTENT:
A Technical Capability represents a functional classification of IT components based on what the technology enables. It describes the core function or purpose of a technology, such as a database, operating system, or server software. Unlike rapidly evolving technologies, the technical capability model remains relatively stable over time and is industry-agnostic.
subject: core_function_or_purpose_of_technology

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Purpose and benefits
CONTENT:
IT components are essential to run, maintain, and change IT products. They may be: • Encapsulated within technical platforms, or • Directly integrated into the IT product. The use of IT components supports strategic architecture management by enabling: • Lifecycle planning of technical assets • Clear documentation of ownership, responsibilities, and competencies • Transparency in the technical landscape • Identification of redundancies (overengineerin
subject: strategic_architecture_management

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Governance and Ownership
CONTENT:
• There is no central authority for managing Objectives.
• Responsibility for creation, maintenance, and usage lies with the creator and user.
• For guidance, contact the Enterprise Architects of the respective portfolio or the Chief Architects.
1. Objective (pink) – Represents a strategic goal.
2. Initiative (green) – A time-bound effort to implement a goal.
3. Organization (yellow) – An organizational unit responsible for the objective.
subject: Initiative

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Key Characteristics of an IT Component
CONTENT:
• It is a hardware or software unit with a clearly defined and dedicated use.
• It can be installed, enabled, disabled, or removed from an operational perspective.
• It represents the smallest modelable unit that forms part of an information system.
• It can be independently modified, distributed, or replaced by third parties.
• It has a managed vendor or manufacturer support lifecycle.
• It has a known vendor, manufacturer, or devel
subject: IT_Component

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Relationships to Other Vendor Fact Sheets
CONTENT:
• Parent-Child Relationships Vendors can be linked to other vendors using parent-child relationships to model corporate hierarchies. However, this is rarely useful in practice. Recommendation: Avoid modeling parent-child relationships unless there is a clear business need. • Successor/Predecessor Relationships Use this relationship to document company renaming or legal succession.
subject: Vendors

CONTENT:

subject: Process

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Definition: IT Component
CONTENT:
An IT Component is a foundational element within the technical architecture of an IT product. It operates independently of any business logic or context. IT components can either be: • Commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) products acquired from external vendors, or • Custom-developed software created in-house. They serve as the technical building blocks that enable the functionality and operation of IT products.
subject: IT_Component

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Responsibility at the Organizational Structure Level
CONTENT:
• Division • Main Department • Department • Subdepartment (lowest level) In LeanIX modeling, unless explicitly stated otherwise, responsibility must always be assigned to the lowest available level of the organizational structure.
• Ensure that the assigned unit reflects the actual operational responsibility.
• This ensures clear accountability, accurate reporting, and effective governance.
subject: Organizational_Structure_Level

source_text_snippet:
SECTION_TITLE: Definition: Initiative
CONTENT:
An Initiative represents a targeted, time-bound effort that drives change within the organization. It requires dedicated resources, impacts the enterprise architecture, and contributes directly to the achievement of one or more defined objectives. Initiatives serve as the operational link between strategic intent and execution, enabling measurable progress toward business goals.
subject: Initiative
